summary:
• Venezuela's oil exports surging to seven-year highs represent a significant supply increase that could pressure Western Hemisphere crude prices and challenge U.S. sanctions enforcement in the region.

• Geopolitical de-escalation with Iran, including potential deal frameworks and ConocoPhillips' Syria engagement, threatens to materially increase Middle Eastern crude supply and weigh on global oil prices.

• Reduced Chinese refinery runs and crude import collapse signal weakening demand from the world's largest importer, creating downside pressure on global crude valuations and Western Hemisphere export economics.
